buggy_code:
import pandas as pd

tick_data = pd.read_csv('tick_data.csv', parse_dates=['timestamp'])

# Compute midpoint price
tick_data['mid'] = (tick_data['bid'] + tick_data['ask']) / 2

# Simulate fills at midpoint — unrealistic
tick_data['fill_price'] = tick_data['mid']

# Strategy returns computed from midpoint fills
tick_data['returns'] = tick_data['fill_price'].pct_change()
clean_code:
import pandas as pd

tick_data = pd.read_csv('tick_data.csv', parse_dates=['timestamp'])

# Buy orders fill at ASK (you pay the ask)
# Sell orders fill at BID (you receive the bid)
tick_data['fill_price_buy'] = tick_data['ask']
tick_data['fill_price_sell'] = tick_data['bid']

# Spread cost per round-trip
tick_data['spread'] = tick_data['ask'] - tick_data['bid']
tick_data['spread_bps'] = (tick_data['spread'] / tick_data['mid']) * 10000

print(f'Average spread: {tick_data["spread_bps"].mean():.1f} bps')
description: Trade fills simulated at bid-ask midpoint rather than realistic ask (for buys) or bid (for sells).
explanation: The midpoint is never an actual fillable price — it exists between bid and ask. Buy orders must cross the spread and fill at the ask. Sell orders fill at the bid. Simulating fills at the midpoint understates transaction costs by half the spread. For high-frequency strategies, this difference alone can make a profitable backtest unprofitable in live trading.
suggested_fix: Fill buy orders at ask price and sell orders at bid price to correctly model transaction costs.

issues.title: Fills simulated at midpoint instead of bid/ask prices
